---
title: "Results"
author: "abi :)"
date: "2026-04-03"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
library(tidyverse)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(readr)     
library(ggplot2)   
library(dplyr)     
library(stringr)   
library(fs)        
```
# GROWTH MEDIA COMPARASION 
```{r}
path_lb <- "/home/abigaylmontantearenas/Documents/proyecto_tesis/04_resultados/rizo/biomass/individuales/lb"
path_lb10 <- "/home/abigaylmontantearenas/Documents/proyecto_tesis/04_resultados/rizo/biomass/individuales/lb10"

biomass_files_lb <- sort(list.files(path = path_lb, pattern = "prokka_carveme_lb_lb\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE))
biomass_files_lb10 <- sort(list.files(path = path_lb10, pattern = "prokka_carveme_lb_lb10\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE))

output_folder <- "/home/abigaylmontantearenas/Documents/proyecto_tesis/04_resultados/rizo/graph"
dir_create(output_folder)

# 2. Dictionaries
colores_bac <- c(
  STECOLI = "#00FF00", 
  ST00060 = "#D4807C", 
  ST00094 = "#C76662", 
  ST00101 = "#5F9EAD", 
  ST00110 = "#B7464C", 
  ST00164 = "#9E3345", 
  ST00143 = "#752530", 
  ST00042 = "#80B6B3", 
  ST00109 = "#3D788E", 
  ST00154 = "#26526B", 
  ST00046 = "#1A3749"
)

name_bac <- c(
  STECOLI = "Escherichia coli", 
  ST00060 = "Arthrobacter sp.",
  ST00094 = "Rhodococcus erythropolis", 
  ST00101 = "Pseudomonas sp.",
  ST00110 = "Variovorax paradoxus", 
  ST00164 = "Bacillus thuringiensis",
  ST00143 = "Paenibacillus sp.", 
  ST00042 = "Pseudomonas umsongensis",
  ST00109 = "Mycobacterium sp.", 
  ST00154 = "Agrobacterium sp.",
  ST00046 = "Bacillus sp."
)

# 3. Loop 
for (i in seq_along(biomass_files_lb)) {
  
  # Extract ID
  model_id <- str_extract(basename(biomass_files_lb[i]), "^[^_]+")
  
  if (!model_id %in% names(colores_bac)) {
    warning(paste("ID not found in dictionary, skipping:", model_id))
    next
  }
  
  # LB data
  df_lb <- read_csv(biomass_files_lb[i], show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
    mutate(time_h = seq(0, by = 0.1, length.out = n()),
           biomass = .[[2]],
           Condition = "LB") %>%
    select(time_h, biomass, Condition)

  # LB/10 data
  df_lb10 <- read_csv(biomass_files_lb10[i], show_col_types = FALSE) %>%
    mutate(time_h = seq(0, by = 0.1, length.out = n()),
           biomass = .[[2]],
           Condition = "LB_10%") %>%
    select(time_h, biomass, Condition)

  # Combine
  df_plot <- bind_rows(df_lb, df_lb10)
  
  # 4. Generate Plot
  p <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = time_h, y = biomass, linetype = Condition)) +
    geom_line(linewidth = 1.2, color = colores_bac[model_id]) +
    scale_linetype_manual(values = c("LB" = "solid", "LB_10%" = "dotted")) +
    labs(
      title = paste(name_bac[model_id]),
      subtitle = paste("Model ID:", model_id),
      x = "Time (h)",
      y = expression(paste(italic("Biomass"), " (gr)"))
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold.italic"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      legend.position = "bottom"
    )
  
  # 5. Save
  output_file <- file.path(output_folder, paste0("comparison_media_", model_id, ".png"))
  
  ggsave(
    filename = output_file,
    plot = p,
    width = 8,
    height = 6,
    dpi = 300
  )
}
```
# INDIVIDUAL BIOMASS CURVES

```{r}
biomass_files <- sort(
  list.files(
    path = "/home/abigaylmontantearenas/Documents/proyecto_tesis/04_resultados/rizo/biomass/individuales/lb",
    pattern = "prokka_carveme_lb_lb\\.csv$", 
    full.names = TRUE
  )
)

output_folder <- "/home/abigaylmontantearenas/Documents/proyecto_tesis/04_resultados/rizo/graph"
#output_file <- file.path(output_folder, "individual_growth_curves_lb10.png")

ggsave(
  filename = output_file,
  plot = p_total,
  width = 12,
  height = 8,
  dpi = 300
)
dir_create(output_folder)

# loop 
for (input_file in biomass_files) {
  

  model_id <- str_extract(basename(input_file), "^[^_]+")
  
  if (!model_id %in% names(colores_bac)) {
    warning(paste("ID not found in dictionary, skipping:", model_id))
    next
  }
  
  # data
  df <- read_csv(input_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
  
  growth_data <- tibble(
    time_h  = seq(0, by = 0.1, length.out = nrow(df)), 
    biomass = df[[2]]
  )
  
  # graph 
  p <- ggplot(growth_data, aes(x = time_h, y = biomass)) +
    geom_line(
      linewidth = 1.3,
      color = colores_bac[model_id]
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste(
        "Individual Growth Curve",
        name_bac[model_id]
      ),
      x = "Time (h)",
      y = expression(paste(italic("Biomass"), " (g)"))
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold.italic"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
  
  #  save
  output_file <- file.path(
    output_folder,
    paste0("individual_growth_", model_id, "prokka_carveme_lb_lb.png")
  )
  
  ggsave(
    filename = output_file,
    plot = p,
    width = 10,
    height = 7,
    dpi = 300
  )
}
```
# INDIVIDUAL BIOMASS CURVES (just one graph)
ocupo seleccionar los renglones correspondiente a las horas, ahorita me esta graficando todos los ciclos

```{r}
biomass_files <- sort(
  list.files(
    path = "/home/abigaylmontantearenas/Documents/proyecto_tesis/04_resultados/rizo/biomass/individuales/lb10",
    pattern = "prokka_carveme_lb_lb10\\.csv$", 
    full.names = TRUE
  )
)

output_folder <- "/home/abigaylmontantearenas/Documents/proyecto_tesis/04_resultados/rizo/graph"
dir_create(output_folder)


all_growth_data <- map_df(biomass_files, function(input_file) {
  
  model_id <- str_extract(basename(input_file), "^[^_]+")
  
  if (!model_id %in% names(colores_bac)) {
    warning(paste("ID not found in dictionary, skipping:", model_id))
    return(NULL) # Salta este archivo si no hay color asignado
  }
  
  df <- read_csv(input_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
  
  tibble(
    time_h   = seq(0, by = 0.1, length.out = nrow(df)), 
    biomass  = df[[2]],
    cepa     = model_id,
    nombre   = name_bac[model_id]
  )
})


p_total <- ggplot(all_growth_data, aes(x = time_h, y = biomass, color = cepa)) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.1, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_manual(
    values = colores_bac, 
    labels = name_bac,
    name = "Cepas"
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Growth Curves Comparison (48 h)",
    x = "Time (h)",
    y = expression(paste(italic("Biomass"), " (g)"))
  ) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.text = element_text(face = "italic"),
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
  )

output_file <- file.path(output_folder, "individual_growth_curves_lb10.png")

ggsave(
  filename = output_file,
  plot = p_total,
  width = 12,
  height = 8,
  dpi = 300
)
```
# COMPARE MASS INDIVUAL VS COMUNIDAD
```{r}
# 1. Asegurarse de que la carpeta de salida exista
output_folder <- "/home/abigaylmontantearenas/Documents/proyecto_tesis/04_resultados/rizo/graph"

# 1. Asegurarse de que la carpeta de salida exista
if (!dir.exists(output_folder)) {
  dir.create(output_folder, recursive = TRUE)
}

# 2. Bucle de graficación
for (nombre_col in colnames(df_comunidad)) {
  
  if (nombre_col %in% names(individuales_list)) {
    
    print(paste("Graficando y guardando:", nombre_col))
    
    # Extraer datos
    bio_comun <- df_comunidad[[nombre_col]]
    # Asegúrate de que "biomass" sea el nombre exacto de la columna en tus CSV individuales
    bio_indiv <- individuales_list[[nombre_col]]$biomass 
    
    # Crear dataframe temporal
    plot_data <- data.frame(
      Valor = c(bio_indiv, bio_comun),
      Condicion = c(rep("Individual", length(bio_indiv)), 
                    rep("Comunidad", length(bio_comun)))
    )
    
    # Generar el gráfico
    p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Condicion, y = Valor, fill = Condicion)) +
      geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
      geom_jitter(width = 0.1, alpha = 0.5) + # Agregué puntos para ver la distribución mejor
      theme_minimal() +
      scale_fill_manual(values = c("Individual" = "#26526B", "Comunidad" = "#B7464C")) +
      labs(title = paste("Comparación Biomasa:", nombre_col),
           subtitle = "Modelo LB10 - Resultados de Tesis",
           y = "Tasa de Crecimiento / Biomasa",
           x = "Condición")
    
    # 3. Definir nombre del archivo dinámicamente
    # Esto creará archivos como "biomasa_Bacteroides.png"
    output_file <- file.path(output_folder, paste0("biomasa_", nombre_col, ".png"))

    # 4. Guardar usando el objeto correcto 'p'
    ggsave(
      filename = output_file,
      plot = p,
      width = 8,   # Un ancho de 8 suele ser mejor para boxplots individuales
      height = 6,
      dpi = 300
    )
    
    # Mostrar en la consola de RStudio
    print(p) 
  }
}






```
# FINAL PRODUCTIVYTI 
```{r}






```


# Compare mets 

```{r}

mets_files <- sort(
  list.files(
    path = "/home/abigaylmontantearenas/Documents/proyecto_tesis/04_resultados/rizo/mets/individuales",
    pattern = "_mets\\.csv$", 
    full.names = TRUE
  )
)

output_folder <- "/home/abigaylmontantearenas/Documents/proyecto_tesis/04_resultados/rizo/graph"
if (!dir.exists(output_folder)) dir.create(output_folder, recursive = TRUE)

# Loop 
for (input_file in mets_files) {
  
  # Extract ID
  model_id <- str_extract(basename(input_file), "^[^_]+")
  
  if (!model_id %in% names(colores_bac)) {
    warning(paste("ID not found in dictionary, skipping:", model_id))
    next
  }
  
  df <- read_csv(input_file, show_col_types = FALSE)
  
  plot_df <- tibble(
    time_h  = seq(0, by = 0.1, length.out = nrow(df)), 
    concentration = df[[2]]  
  )
  
  # plot
  p <- ggplot(plot_df, aes(x = time_h, y = concentration)) +
    geom_line(
      linewidth = 1.3,
      color = colores_bac[model_id]
    ) +
    labs(
      title = paste("Metabolite Profile –", name_bac[model_id]),
      subtitle = paste("Model:", model_id),
      x = "Time (h)",
      y = "Concentration (mmol/L)" # Adjust units as necessary for your GEMs
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 14) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold.italic"),
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank()
    )
  
  # Save
  output_file <- file.path(output_folder, paste0("individual_mets_", model_id, ".png"))
  
  ggsave(
    filename = output_file,
    plot = p,
    width = 10,
    height = 7,
    dpi = 300
  )
}
```



```{r}
library(tidyverse)

# 1. Load the data
df <- read_csv("/home/abigaylmontantearenas/Documents/proyecto_tesis/04_resultados/rizo/mets/individuales/ST00042_mets.csv")

# 2. Reshape and Plot
df %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -1, names_to = "Metabolite", values_to = "Value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = .[[1]], y = Value, color = Metabolite)) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(x = "Time", title = "Metabolite Concentrations") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
df1 <- read.csv("/home/abigaylmontantearenas/Documents/proyecto_tesis/04_resultados/rizo/biomass/individuales/lb10/ST00042_prokka_carveme_lb_lb10.csv") 
df1_42 <- df1[2:82, ]
df2 <- read.csv("/home/abigaylmontantearenas/Documents/proyecto_tesis/04_resultados/rizo/biomass_comunidad_8/comunidad_1.csv")
df3 <- read.csv("/home/abigaylmontantearenas/Documents/proyecto_tesis/04_resultados/rizo/biomass_comunidad_8/comunidad_3.csv")
df2_42 <- df2$ST00042


# 1. Extraer la última fila (la biomasa final)
final_values <- as.numeric(combined_df[nrow(combined_df), ])
final_values
column_names <- c("Individual", "Comunidad 1", "Comunidad 3")

# 2. Crear un nuevo data frame organizado para ggplot
df_plot <- data.frame(
  Condicion = column_names,
  Biomasa = final_values
)

# 3. Generar la gráfica
library(ggplot2)

ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = Condicion, y = Biomasa, fill = Condicion)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", color = "black", width = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") + # Colores profesionales
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    title = "Comparación de Biomasa Final: ST00042",
    subtitle = "Modelos individuales vs. comunidades",
    x = "Condición de Crecimiento",
    y = "Biomasa (unidades de flujo)" 
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "none") # Quitamos la leyenda porque el eje X ya lo dice
#######################################
# Define the path
# 1. Update this line to include the full path
files <- list.files("/home/abigaylmontantearenas/Documents/proyecto_tesis/04_resultados/rizo/biomass/individuales/lb10/", 
                    pattern = "\\.csv$", 
                    full.names = TRUE)
id <- list()
# 2. Now your loop will work because 'i' will be the full path
for (i in files) {
  name <- basename(i)
  id <- sub("_.*", "", name)
  
  # Now R knows exactly where to find the file
  temp_data <- read.csv(i)
  
  # Just a suggestion: print the ID to see the progress
  print(paste(id))
}

id
for nam in id
if which(colnames(df) == "target_name"
         
#######################
# 1. Preparar rutas y archivos
indi_path <- "/home/abigaylmontantearenas/Documents/proyecto_tesis/04_resultados/rizo/biomass/individuales/lb10/"
comm_path <- "/home/abigaylmontantearenas/Documents/proyecto_tesis/04_resultados/rizo/biomass_comunidad_8/"

indi_files <- list.files(indi_path, pattern = "\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)
comm_files <- list.files(comm_path, pattern = "\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)

# 2. Crear un objeto vacío para guardar los resultados
# Usaremos una lista porque es más rápido que "pegar" columnas una por una
lista_columnas <- list()

for (i in indi_files) {
  id_archivo <- sub("_.*", "", basename(i))
  
  for (c in comm_files) {
    df_comm <- read.csv(c)
    
    # 3. Si el ID es una columna, guardamos esa columna
    if (id_archivo %in% colnames(df_comm)) {
      message("Extrayendo columna: ", id_archivo, " desde ", basename(c))
      
      # Guardamos la columna en nuestra lista usando el ID como nombre
      lista_columnas[[id_archivo]] <- df_comm[[id_archivo]]
      
      # Si ya lo encontraste en un archivo de comunidad, 
      # puedes usar 'break' para no seguir buscando en los demás .csv de comunidad
      break 
    }
  }
}

# 4. Convertir la lista en un dataframe final
# Nota: Esto solo funciona si todas las columnas tienen la misma longitud (mismo número de filas)
df_final <- as.data.frame(lista_columnas)

# Ver el resultado
head(df_final)



         
         
```
